Excellent problem‑solving, communication, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to articulate technical concepts clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences. Proficiency in Microsoft technologies, including Active Directory, Certificate Authority (PKI), Group Policy, and other related Microsoft infrastructure services. Willing to be on 24x7 standby Possess valid class 3 license is a plus point.    Work location: Jurong East

Plan and execute machinery condition monitoring and field data collection with production team and engineers. Ensure all plant valves are fully operational and all mechanical components properly grease and lubricated where applicable, including carry out, strip, redress and rebuild of various types of control and plant valves. REQUIREMENTS  The candidate must possess a Professional Certificate/NiTEC, Diploma in Engineering (Mechanical/ Mechatronic/ Electromechanical) or equivalent. At least 3 years of practical experience in the oil and petrochemical refinery. Experience in hands-on troubleshooting of mechanical/ machinery systems i.e compressor/pump and diesel/gas engine. Familiar with Permit to Work system and local codes, regulations and standards requirement. Proficient in usage of Microsoft Office and trained in the use of the Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS); ie SAP. Ability to speak, write and communicate effectively in English. High self-motivation with the ability to work with minimal supervision & adapt to changing priorities and assignments. Willing to take up new challenges and responsibilities.

Job DescriptionWe are seeking a meticulous and proactive Compliance Officer to join our Regulatory Compliance team. In this critical role, you will be responsible for upholding our Major Payment Institution (MPI) licence obligations and ensuring adherence to all relevant MAS regulations in Singapore. a Bachelor’s degree or professional compliance certification is a strong advantage.· 3-5 years of relevant compliance experience within a bank or MAS-regulated financial institution (payments industry experience highly preferred).· Proven experience in transaction monitoring, STR/SAR reporting, and ML/CFT risk assessment.· Candidates with less experience but exceptional analytical and stakeholder management skills will be considered.Knowledge & Skills:· Knowledge of Singapore’s regulatory landscape, particularly Payments Services Act requirements, AML/CFT, and KYC/CDD.· Proficiency with regulatory reporting portals (e.g., SONAR, MASNET) and MS Office.· Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and report-writing skills.· Strong interpersonal and communication abilities, with proficiency in written and spoken English. Company Profile

About CapBridge
CapBridge, a member of FOMO Group, is a leading digital investment platform headquartered in Singapore. As a Capital Markets Services licensee, CapBridge is regulated by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) to offer top-tier funds, digital asset funds, stocks, bonds, equities, and arrange life insurance products. Job Description

About the Role
We are looking for a highly motivated Trustee & Custody Intern to support our operations across trust administration, custody oversight, and digital asset lifecycle management. This role is ideal for someone interested in capital markets, asset servicing, SPV structures, digital securities, and MAS-regulated operations. You will work closely with the operations, compliance, legal, and product teams to support day-to-day trustee and custody processes, contribute to regulatory compliance, and assist in ensuring proper safeguarding of client and issuer assets.
Key Responsibilities
1. Custody Operations Support
• Assist with daily reconciliation of cash, securities, and digital assets
• Support the settlement and tracking of asset movements and corporate actions
• Prepare and maintain accurate custody records, reports, and audit trails
• Verify transaction instructions under supervision (issuance, redemption, transfers)
2. Trustee Administration & Oversight
• Review trust deeds, SPV documents, and issuer submissions
• Support ongoing monitoring of issuer obligations and covenant compliance
• Assist with preparing trustee consents, attestations, resolutions, and documentation
• Help track issuer reporting timelines, breaches, or follow-up actions
3. KYC, AML, and Due Diligence
• Assist in onboarding issuers, counterparties, and intermediaries
• Collect and review KYC/KYB documents for AML and regulatory checks
• Perform preliminary screening using AML tools and prepare risk summaries
4. Documentation, Filing & Reporting
• Organize and maintain custody and trustee records for regulatory inspections
• Support the preparation of trustee minutes, board packs, and transaction logs
• Prepare periodic reports on trust/custody activities for senior management
5. Cross-Functional Coordination
• Liaise with internal stakeholders (Compliance, Legal, Product, Operations) to support workflow execution
• Coordinate with external parties (custodian banks, brokers, issuers, auditors) as needed
• Document meeting minutes and follow up on outstanding items
6. Regulatory & Risk Support
• Assist in preparing materials for MAS reviews, audits, or internal checks
• Help monitor asset segregation, exception handling, and risk indicator
• Support updates to SOPs, checklists, and process documentation
7. Research & Project Assistance
• Conduct research on asset servicing best practices, trust structures, and digital asset operations
• Support internal projects such as workflow digitisation, system upgrades, and UAT testing
• Contribute to process improvement initiatives
Requirements
Must-Have
• Current undergraduate pursuing Business, Finance, Accounting, Economics, Law, or related fields
• Strong attention to detail and commitment to accuracy
• Good analytical and organisational skills
• Ability to handle confidential information with discretion
• Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, Word, and basic reporting tools
Nice-to-Have
• Interest in capital markets, securities operations, SPV/trust structures, or tokenization
• Knowledge of MAS regulations (SFA, FAA, Trust Companies Act) is a bonus
• Experience in operations, compliance, finance clubs, or related internships
What You Will Gain
• Exposure to MAS-regulated trustee and custody operations
• Experience with digital securities, SPV structures, and tokenized assets
• Hands-on learning across compliance, legal, operations, and product workflows
• Opportunity to contribute to process improvements in a fast-growing fintech
• Mentorship from experienced professionals in capital markets and fintech

Job Title: Personal Financial Consultant - Global Consumer Financial Services
Job Description: Personal Financial Consultant - Global Consumer Financial Services OCBC opens doors to new opportunities.Start your career with OCBC as a Personal Financial Consultant!At OCBC we are constantly on the lookout for exceptional individuals to join our team. As Singapore’s longest established local bank, we have a global network of more than 450 branches and representative offices in more than 15 countries and territories. This is not a typical 9–5 job, and it’s definitely not a typical career path. This is an opportunity for ambitious, driven young individuals who want more — more freedom, more income, and more control over their future.As a Wealth Manager, you’ll be building a high-earning, future-proof career in finance while enjoying real flexibility over your time. This role is designed for people with a burning passion for success, personal growth, and helping others make smarter financial decisions. If you’re motivated, people-focused, and hungry to build something meaningful, you’ll thrive here.You won’t be clock-watching or stuck behind a desk all day. Instead, you’ll have the freedom to manage your own schedule and design your working day around your lifestyle. Whether you’re balancing studies, travel, family, or personal goals, this role rewards results, not hours worked. Performance matters — and when you perform, you’re rewarded.And yes, the earning potential is very real. This is a high-income opportunity with uncapped upside. Your earnings are performance-based, meaning the harder and smarter you work, the more you earn. There is no ceiling here — top performers build exceptional incomes and long-term financial security. Your ambition directly determines your success.In this role, you’ll work closely with clients to help them plan, grow, and protect their wealth. You’ll build lasting relationships, develop your own client base, and gain hands-on experience in real-world wealth management. Along the way, you’ll sharpen your communication skills, business mindset, and financial expertise while building a career with genuine ownership and long-term potential.You don’t need years of experience to apply. What matters most is mindset. We’re looking for individuals who are ambitious, coachable, driven, and eager to learn. If you’re passionate about finance, personal development, and achieving above-average results, this opportunity was built for you. Graduates, career starters, and motivated career changers are all encouraged to apply.You won’t be doing this alone. Full training, ongoing development, and mentorship from experienced professionals are provided, along with a supportive, high-performance environment that pushes you to be your best. If you’re willing to put in the effort, you’ll be given the tools and support to succeed.If you’re tired of income limits, rigid schedules, and careers that don’t excite you, this is your chance to do something different. A career as a Wealth Manager offers flexibility, financial freedom, and the opportunity to build a future on your own terms.This isn’t just a job — it’s a career move that can change your life.

Job Title: 反欺诈调查经理 | Fraud Investigation Manager
Company: HAIER SINGAPORE INVESTMENT HOLDING PTE. LTD.
Job Description: 公司背景 | Company BackgroundHaier 是一家全球化、多元化经营的集团型企业，业务覆盖多个国家和行业，持续推进国际化与规模化发展。随着业务复杂度和跨区域经营不断提升，集团对廉洁合规、风险防控及反舞弊治理提出了更高要求。为进一步强化集团反舞弊体系建设，提升重点领域和平台的风险识别与治理能力，支持业务长期、合规、健康发展，特设立本岗位。Haier is a diversified global enterprise with operations across multiple countries and industries, continuously advancing international expansion and business scale. As business complexity and cross-regional operations increase, the Group has raised higher requirements for integrity, compliance, risk prevention, and anti-fraud governance.To further strengthen the Group’s anti-fraud framework, enhance risk identification and governance capabilities across key domains and platforms, and support long-term, compliant, and healthy business development, this position is established.主要职责 | Key Responsibilities承接集团交办的重点舞弊案件，识别涉案内部人员及外部合作方，推动损失挽回与责任追究，形成震慑效应，支持业务的合规与健康发展。推动反舞弊工作在重点领域和产业的平台化、体系化落地，从业绩差距和长期低效平台切入，系统性解决深层次舞弊与治理问题。搭建并持续优化区域反舞弊管理体系，确保符合当地法律法规及集团制度并有效落地。建立并推广标准化、多语言的舞弊举报渠道，覆盖各业务单元，强化诚信文化与合规意识。负责舞弊案件的闭环管理，持续提升调查质量与效率。以案件为抓手识别流程与内控缺陷，协同业务推动整改方案设计与落地，防止同类风险反复发生。负责反舞弊流程制度体系建设、举报投诉渠道的宣贯，舞弊线索收集，廉洁文化建设及警示教育培训。搭建并优化反舞弊管理与协同机制，加强与总部及集团各部门的沟通协作，提升集团整体反舞弊合力。Take the lead on key fraud cases assigned by Headquarters, identify internal personnel and external partners involved, facilitate loss recovery and accountability, enhance deterrence, and support compliant and sustainable business development.Drive the implementation of anti-fraud efforts across key domains and industries, focusing on performance gaps and long-term underperforming platforms, and addressing entrenched issues through systematic anti-fraud initiatives. Build and continuously optimize the regional anti-fraud management framework in compliance with local laws and Group policies, ensuring effective implementation. Establish and promote standardized, multilingual whistleblowing channels across business units to strengthen integrity culture and compliance awareness.Manage fraud investigations and closed-loop case handling, including effectiveness assessment, to continuously improve investigation quality and efficiency.Leverage fraud cases to identify underlying process and control weaknesses, work with business teams to design and implement corrective actions, and prevent recurrence of similar risks.Be responsible for the establishment of anti-fraud process and system framework, promotion of whistleblowing and complaint channels, collection of fraud-related information, integrity culture building, and implementation of warning education and training programs.Build and optimize anti-fraud governance and collaboration mechanisms, and coordinate closely with relevant departments across the Group to enhance overall anti-fraud effectiveness.岗位要求 | Job Requirements本科及以上学历，法律、审计、会计、财务、风险管理或相关专业优先。具备 8 年以上 反舞弊（包括内部贪腐或受贿）、合规、内部审计或相关法律领域的工作经验，具有跨国或区域经验者优先。具备企业内部员工舞弊、贪腐、受贿等案件的实务调查与处理经验，不限于销售端反商业贿赂，能够独立或主导内部调查工作；优先考虑具备甲方制造业背景者，熟悉制造型企业从采购、研发、生产、销售、物流及供应链管理等全业务流程，对各环节常见舞弊与廉洁风险有系统性认知及实操应对经验；具备将内部调查案件移送司法机关并推进刑事追责的实际经验，熟悉刑民交叉、证据固定及与司法机关沟通流程；具备法律教育或法律相关工作背景者优先（如法学、合规、内控、审计、纪检监察等）。具备独立开展舞弊调查、风险识别与分析的能力。具备制度与流程建设能力，能够推动反舞弊管理体系的落地与执行。具备实际舞弊案件调查及司法处理相关经验。具备良好的组织能力、沟通协调能力及团队协作精神，具备问题识别与前瞻性判断能力，积极主动，愿意接受工作挑战。英语及普通话可作为工作语言，具备良好的跨文化、跨部门沟通及汇报能力。持有 CFE 等相关专业证书者优先。可接受必要的全球出差。Bachelor’s degree or above, preferably in Law, Auditing, Accounting, Finance, Risk Management, or related fields.At least 8 years of experience in anti-fraud (including internal corruption or bribery), compliance, internal audit, or related legal roles, with multinational or regional experience preferred.Proven experience handling internal employee corruption/bribery cases within enterprises; experience limited only to sales-side anti-commercial bribery is not sufficient.Preferably has a background on the owner/client side in the manufacturing industry, with understanding of fraud and corruption risks and hands-on handling experience across the full business process, including procurement, R&D, sales, logistics, and the supply chain.Practical experience in transferring cases to judicial authorities for criminal prosecution; a legal background is preferred.Strong ability to independently conduct fraud investigations, risk identification, and analysis.Proven capability in designing and implementing anti-fraud policies, systems, and processes.Hands-on experience in fraud case investigations and related legal or judicial proceedings.Strong organizational, communication, coordination, and team collaboration skills, with the ability to identify issues and exercise forward-looking judgment, and a proactive mindset with willingness to take on work challenges.Fluent in English and Mandarin as working languages, with strong cross-cultural and cross-functional communication and reporting skills.Relevant professional certifications, such as Certified Fraud Examiner (CFE), are preferred.Willing to travel globally as required.

Job Title: KYC Analyst / Junior KYC Analyst - Compliance
Job Description: KYC Analyst / Junior KYC Analyst – Compliance (Asia-Pacific) Position Overview A globally active trading, brokerage, and logistics-focused organisation is seeking a KYC Analyst to join its regional Compliance team based in Asia. This role plays a key part in supporting the firm’s counterparty risk framework across multiple jurisdictions, working closely with front office, management, and global compliance colleagues. The successful candidate will be responsible for managing customer due diligence, onboarding, periodic reviews, and ongoing monitoring, ensuring regulatory and internal compliance standards are consistently met in a fast-paced, international environment. Key Responsibilities  Manage and coordinate end-to-end client onboarding, ensuring a smooth transition from onboarding to active client status. Conduct AML/KYC due diligence on corporate clients across a range of legal structures and jurisdictions, including onboarding, periodic reviews, and trigger event reviews. Collect, assess, verify, and maintain KYC documentation in line with internal policies and regulatory requirements. Perform screening for sanctions, politically exposed persons (PEPs), and adverse media, identifying, assessing, and escalating risks where appropriate. Clearly document due diligence findings, risk assessments, and any deviations from standard documentation, including proposed mitigations. Classify clients according to risk and regulatory profiles and monitor for material changes impacting client risk status. Liaise directly with brokers, front office teams, management, and external counterparties to resolve KYC and onboarding queries efficiently. Investigate and escalate suspicious activity and potential financial crime issues, including AML and fraud-related concerns. Maintain accurate records, trackers, and databases relating to KYC, sanctions, PEPs, and adverse media. Provide timely and clear progress updates to relevant stakeholders, both verbally and in writing. Take ownership of assigned cases, managing them through to resolution while meeting internal service level expectations. Support broader compliance initiatives, projects, and training requirements as directed by regional or global compliance leadership. Contribute to a strong risk-aware and compliance-focused culture across the business.  Qualifications & Experience  2–5 years of experience in KYC, AML, compliance, or client onboarding within financial services, brokerage, trading, or a related environment. Solid understanding of KYC regulations, customer risk assessment, sanctions screening, and adverse media processes. Experience handling corporate structures across multiple jurisdictions; exposure to higher-risk or complex entities is an advantage. Familiarity with regulatory expectations in Singapore and awareness of international regulatory frameworks (e.g. UK, EU, US) is beneficial. Strong analytical skills with excellent attention to detail and sound judgment. Effective written and verbal communication skills, with confidence engaging internal and external stakeholders. Proficiency in Microsoft Office and standard business systems.  Key Competencies & Attributes  Proactive, organised, and solution-oriented, with the ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities. Strong sense of integrity, discretion, and professionalism when handling sensitive information. Collaborative team player who can also operate effectively with minimal supervision. Comfortable working in a fast-paced, international, and evolving regulatory environment. Adaptable, resilient, and committed to continuous learning and professional development.  Jan Enevold (EA Reg no: R22106593)Faststream Recruitment Pte Ltd, EA Licence no: 09C3403

JOB SUMMARY
Tan Tock Seng Hospital invites applications for a Research Assistant in the Department of Epidemiology and Preventive Medicine. This department is led by a team of experienced Public Health Specialists and is deeply committed to research in population health, communicable and non-communicable disease epidemiology, socio-behavioural sciences, and preventive medicine.
The job will be part of a team of clinical, allied health and research staff who are interested in understanding loneliness in older adults and the role of nature-based activities in addressing loneliness. Under the supervision of the Principal Investigator, the Research Assistant will provide support for the research study by using both quantitative and qualitative methods to collect data for the study and facilitate workshops while ensuring proper conduct and implementation of all research activities. The Research Assistant will work closely with the Principal Investigator (PI) and study team members on research-related duties to ensure successful translation of research findings into community health practice and policy.
MAIN DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
SPECIFIC 

Work with a multidisciplinary research team to design and implement mixed methods studies and community-based interventions in the area of preventive medicine.
Recruit, liaise with, and collect data from research participants.
Conduct in-depth interviews, facilitate co-design workshop sessions, and coordinate community-based interventions.
Organize and manage both qualitative and quantitative data, ensuring data quality and validation.
Support intervention implementation, monitoring, and evaluation activities.
Analyze, interpret and integrate both qualitative and quantitative data generated from the study.
Support literature reviews, conference presentations, and manuscript preparation as required.

GENERAL 

Ensure adherence to study protocols, ethical requirements, and standard operating procedures throughout all research activities.
Manage complete and accurate documentation for Institutional Review Board submissions and amendments.
Manage research documentation and ensure audit compliance.
Prepare for site visits, audits, etc. by ensuring that all study files are complete and up to date.
Coordinate with community partners and stakeholders for successful project implementation.
Support procurement of research materials and management of project resources.
Perform other duties as directed by the HOD and Principal Investigator.

JOB REQUIREMENTS
(A) EDUCATION AND TRAINING
Minimum degree in relevant healthcare discipline (e.g. Nursing, Pharmacy, Psychology, Social Work, Gerontology) OR general degree with demonstrated interest in community-based research.
(B) EXPERIENCE
Minimum 1-2 years of research experience
Experience in working with older adults or community-based projects is preferred but not mandatory
Competent oral and written English communication skills
Oral and written proficiency in local languages (e.g Chinese, Malay, Chinese dialects) is preferred but not mandatory
Astute critical thinking skills and ability to work independently and in a team
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills and ability to work with older adults
Proficient in MS Office applications (Word, Excel, Powerpoint)
Proficiency in softwares such as SPSS, Stata, Nvivo preferred but not mandatory
Strong initiative, willingness to perform duties and learn new skills as and when required

Job SummaryThe Nuclear Medicine Technologist will be responsible for helping the diagnosis and treatment of certain diseases. They administer radiopharmaceuticals (radioactive drugs) to patients and monitor the concentration levels of the drugs in the patient's body. Technologists work directly with patients under the supervision of a physician.Technologists perform several specific duties. First, they explain the test procedures to patients. Then they prepare a dosage of the radiopharmaceutical, following safety procedures. After administering the drug, the technologists operate a gamma scintillation camera, or scanner, which creates pictures of the drug as it passes through parts of the patient's body. Abnormal areas show higher or lower concentrations of radioactivity than normal. The images are produced on a computer screen or photographic film for a physician to examine. Nuclear medicine technologists must also keep records of patient treatments, equipment use and maintenance, and the amount and type of radiopharmaceuticals received, used, and disposed of.Deliverables Job Title: SCDF Paramedic Specialist (Work-Study Diploma in Paramedicine)
Job Description: What the role isThe Paramedic Specialist (Work-Study Diploma in Paramedicine) programme by SCDF offers an excellent option for individuals navigating the post-ITE graduation phase, providing a blend of academic pursuit and practical work experience, thus offering the benefits of both realms. Radlink / Radlink LiteJob Summary The Radiographer will be responsible for performing in one or more of the following imaging procedures; X-Ray, Fluoroscopy, Mammography, Computer Tomography, Bone Mineral Densitometry, Magnetic Resonance imaging procedures as per his/her professional training in the imaging techniques, with knowledge and skill in the use of ionizing and non-ionizing radiation.Deliverables & ResponsibilitiesOperations & Clinical •      To perform diagnostic imaging procedures on patients as requested by referring clinicians and to assist the clinical aspect of the Centre's business.•       Be clinically competent in the range of diagnostic imaging services he/she is trained in, and offered by the Centre.•       Perform all diagnostic procedures in accordance with clinically accepted standards described in the SOP and relevant work instructions.•      To perform imaging procedureso       Accurately, as per clinical request and to patients’ clinical needs.o       Expeditiously, with attention to patient comfort and Radiation Safety.o      Of Diagnostic relevance for the Radiologist to report.•       Registered imaging requests with the accompanying folder and payment advice form will be place within the respective modality assigned wall slots, from which Radiographers will pick for imaging.•       Select patient to be imaged from the modality worklist on the imaging equipment.•       Ensure that all imaging procedures are performed on the correct patient with regard to the identification and due observance of the relevant policy on irradiation of women of child bearing age and procedural consent. Ensure imaging is done on the correct patient, with correct imaging procedure and technique used. Acquire images with the highest possible image quality with the available imaging equipments used. Acquired images are to be sent to PACs for archive.•       Radiographers are to check and ensure that patients are deemed suitable for the procedure, paying attention to possibility of patient being pregnant when scheduled for X-Ray procedures or the risk of drug allergy.•       Ensure printed films are of optimal quality, in terms of zoom factor, window/width and level. All films are to be correctly labeled and in accordance to the imaging procedure requested, before sending to the Radiologist for reporting or given to patient as wet-film.•       All Radiographers are to START and END each imaging procedure in ERIS.•       Assist in advising and explaining to patients of any additional charges, and to indicate by writing down the additional charges for additional scan/contrast on the Payment Advice Form.•       Instruct all patients to bring the payment advice form to the front counter.•       Give patients appropriate instruction after imaging procedure; To wait for wet-films, to leave after payment, to go back to the clinic etc.•      Assume responsibilities and commitments for the day-to-day operations of the imaging unit in the department.•      Actively seek continual self-education to keep abreast with all new imaging technology, procedures and applications.Patient Care & Public Relation Skills •       To co-operate and work hand-in-hand between Front Office staff and Nurses to ensure patients expeditiously turnaround for the imaging procedure. Work closely with other sections to co-ordinate patient appointments in such a manner as to minimize patient waiting time at the Centre.•       Assist to position patients for CT and MR when there is an absence or shortage of Nurses.•       Provide clear and simple explanation to the patient for each imaging procedure and the estimated duration of the procedure to ally their anxiety and to gain his/her cooperation.•       Provide adequate reassurance whenever appropriate.•       Ensure the safety, physical and emotional well-being of patients both during and after imaging procedure.•       Handle all patients in a polite and emphatic manner.•      Ensure that all patient and clinician complaints are resolved in an amicable manner whenever possible.•      Consult the Radiologist whenever in doubt about any aspect of the requested imaging procedure, so as to minimize incidents of having to recall patients for repeat procedures or additions views.•      Organize the workflow of the examination room so as to enable all procedures to be performed efficiently thereby minimizing the patients' waiting time in the Centre.Other Essential Management Tasks •      Ensure to comply with the relevant policies and procedures and report to the center Manager any serious breach of the same.•      Report to the Centre Manager and document any unusual events or incidents which have occurred at the Centre that could potentially result in an official investigation in compliance with the relevant incident reporting policy.•      Protect the confidentiality of all company proprietary information and patient record per relevant confidentiality policy.Patient Safety & Radiation Protection •      Ensure and perform proper screening procedure for patients undergoing MRI examinations to ascertain that they are not contraindicated for such procedures.•      Take all precautions to protect patients, staff and members of the public from unnecessary exposure to ionizing radiation.•      Be familiar with the recommendations of ICRP with regard to radiation protection and take all necessary steps to ensure compliance.•      Ensure that personal radiation monitoring devices are worn by all radiographers when working with imaging equipments which emits ionizing radiation and the timely renewal of their radiation licenses.•      Ensure and obtain a written consent for all patients undergoing imaging procedures with the administration of intravenous/intramuscular/intracavity/intraarticular administration of contrast media and maintain proper records for such documents for future reference.Care of Equipment & Quality Control •      Ensure and perform daily QA procedures for the imaging equipment to maintain the consistently optimal performance of such equipment.•      Be familiar with the guidelines set out in the center’s Standard Operating Policies and Standard Operating Procedures and comply with them.•      Understand the relevant service culture of the center and values which apply to staff.•      Report to the Senior Clinical Manager of any unusual event or incident that occurred in the center according to relevant policy.•      Protect the confidentiality of all company proprietary information and patient records•      Inform the Senior Clinical Manager, company’s IT manager and the respective vendors' service representatives of any irregularities and breakdown in the performance of the equipment hardware/software.•      Maintain an equipment logbook for each examination room and ensure that all radiographers document all equipment problems, downtime and action taken to rectify such problems in the respective log books.•      Ensure and undertake to maintain the cleanliness and aesthetic appearance of the assigned examination room(s) and perform daily cleaning of the exposed surfaces of the equipment (especially areas in contact with the patient).•      To arrange and coordinate the Preventive Maintenance for all diagnostic equipmentResponsibility & Accountable for the following•      Understand all aspects of Diagnostic Imaging Modalities including Nuclear Medicine and PET imaging and be able to assist in all applicable imaging procedures.•      Assists the Senior Clinical Manager in developing short- and long-term goals and action plan for the unit.•      Able to perform all general radiographic and special fluoroscopic examinations offered by the Centre with a repeat rate of less than 5% (due to human error).•      Able to perform all examinations offered by the Centre in at least one other specialized modality (e.g. MRI, CT. Mammography and Ultrasound).•      To protect the safety and well-being of patients and staff when carrying out all diagnostic imaging procedures.•      To ensure all patients are attended to promptly and efficiently.•      To ensure the proper care and maintenance of all imaging equipment.•      To be responsible for maintaining the quality standards for medical imaging as specified in the RDIS Quality Assurance Plan.•      Performs any other reasonable duties as directed by the Clinical Manager/Chief Radiographer from time to time.•      To perform and undertake any assignment or ad-hoc tasks delegated by Manager from time to time.Requirements Qualifications•      Possesses a Diploma of the College of Radiographers, UK (DCR, UK) or a Diploma in Radiography from Nanyang Polytechnic, Singapore, and/or a Bachelor’s Degree in Applied Science (Radiography) from a reputable academic institution.•      Member of the Singapore Society of Radiographers and/or other local or overseas related professional bodies.•      Minimum of 2 years’ working experience as a radiographer in a hospital-based diagnostic imaging facility.•      Prior experience in managing a section in a hospital-based radiology department (i.e. A&E, Women’s Imaging, Advanced Imaging etc.)Knowledge, Skills and Abilities •      Preferably possesses subspecialty experience in one or more of the following modalities:o      CTo      MRIo      Ultrasoundo      MammographyBy submitting your application, you grant consent to Fullerton Health and affiliates to utilize your information to assess job suitability and be considered for other suitable positions.

Health’s Clinical Quality & Governance (CQG) standards.  Key Responsibilities: Clinical Governance & SOP Development Lead the development, implementation, and continuous review of clinical SOPs for
